title: Order 2023/2 of 27 April 2023
text:
INTERNATIONAL TRIBUNAL FOR THE LAW OF THE SEA
REPORTS OF JUDGMENTS,
ADVISORY OPINIONS AND ORDERS
THE M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (No. 2) CASE
(MARSHALL ISLANDS/EQUATORIAL GUINEA)
List of cases: No. 32
ORDER OF 27 APRIL 2023
2023
TRIBUNAL INTERNATIONAL DU DROIT DE LA MER
RECUEIL DES ARRÊTS,
AVIS CONSULTATIFS ET ORDONNANCES
AFFAIRE DU NAVIRE « HEROIC IDUN » (No. 2)
(ÎLES MARSHALL/GUINÉE ÉQUATORIALE)
Rôle des affaires : No. 32
ORDONNANCE DU 27 AVRIL 2023

M/T “Heroic Idun” (No. 2) (Marshall Islands/Equatorial Guinea),

27 APRIL 2023
ORDER
THE M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (No. 2) CASE
(MARSHALL ISLANDS/EQUATORIAL GUINEA)
AFFAIRE DU NAVIRE « HEROIC IDUN » (No. 2)
(ÎLES MARSHALL/GUINÉE ÉQUATORIALE)
27 AVRIL 2023
ORDONNANCE

213
INTERNATIONAL TRIBUNAL FOR THE LAW OF THE SEA
YEAR 2023
27 April 2023
List of cases:
No. 32
THE M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (No. 2) CASE
(MARSHALL ISLANDS/EQUATORIAL GUINEA)
Constitution of chamber
ORDER
Present: President HOFFMANN; Vice-President HEIDAR; Judges JESUS,
PAWLAK, YANAI, KATEKA, BOUGUETAIA, PAIK, ATTARD, KULYK,
GÓMEZ-ROBLEDO, CABELLO, CHADHA, KITTICHAISAREE,
KOLODKIN, LIJNZAAD, INFANTE CAFFI, DUAN, BROWN,
CARACCIOLO, KAMGA; Registrar HINRICHS OYARCE.
THE TRIBUNAL,
composed as above,
after deliberation,
Having regard to articles 15, paragraphs 2 and 4, 17, 24 and 27 of the Statute
of the Tribunal (hereinafter “the Statute”),

M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (NO. 2) (ORDER 27 APRIL 2023) 214
Having regard to articles 19, 30, 31, 45 and 55 of the Rules of the Tribunal
(hereinafter “the Rules”),
Makes the following Order:
1. Whereas, further to consultations held by the President of the Tribunal
with representatives of the Republic of Marshall Islands (hereinafter “the
Marshall Islands”) and the Republic of Equatorial Guinea (hereinafter
“Equatorial Guinea”) in Hamburg on 18 April 2023, a special agreement was
concluded between the two States on 18 April 2023 to submit to a special
chamber of the Tribunal, to be formed pursuant to article 15, paragraph 2, of
the Statute, “the dispute concerning the M/T “Heroic Idun” and her crew”;
2. Whereas the Special Agreement and Notification between the Marshall
Islands and Equatorial Guinea dated 18 April 2023 (hereinafter “the Special
Agreement”), in its relevant part, reads:
Special Agreement and Notification
Pursuant to article 15, paragraph 2, of the Statute of the Tribunal,
the Republic of the Marshall Islands and the Republic of Equatorial
Guinea hereby record their agreement to submit to a special chamber
of International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea the dispute concern-
ing the M/T “Heroic Idun” and her crew. The agreement was reached on
18 April 2023, under the conditions reflected in the agreed Minutes of
Consultations (18 April 2023), attached hereto.
The Republic of the Marshall Islands and the Republic of Equatorial
Guinea further record their agreement that the special chamber shall be
comprised of the following five individuals:
Judge Albert J. Hoffmann, as President
Judge María Teresa Infante Caffi
Judge Kathy-Ann Brown
Mr Gudmundur Eiriksson, Judge ad hoc (Marshall Islands)
Mr Philippe Couvreur, Judge ad hoc (Equatorial Guinea)

M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (NO. 2) (ORDER 27 APRIL 2023) 215
Delivery on today’s date of an original of this Agreement and Noti-
fication to the Registry of the Tribunal shall constitute the notification
contemplated in article 55 of the Rules of the Tribunal.
Pursuant to article 56, paragraph 3, of the Rules, the Republic of the
Marshall Islands and the Republic of Equatorial Guinea have the honour
to notify the Tribunal that the Government of the Marshall Islands has
appointed H.E. Ms Meredith Kirby, Deputy Commissioner of Maritime
Affairs, as its Agent and the Government of the Republic of Equatorial
Guinea has appointed H.E. Mr Carmelo Nvono-Ncá, Ambassador, as its
Agent, for the purpose of all proceedings in connection with said dispute.
The address[es] for service to which all communications concerning
the case are to be sent in accordance with article 56, paragraph 1, of the
Rules are as follows:
For the Government of the Republic of the Marshall Islands:
International Registries GmbH
Am Kaiserkai 13, 2nd Floor
20457 Hamburg
Germany
…
For the Government of the Republic of Equatorial Guinea:
Embassy of the Republic of Equatorial Guinea
Rohlfsstraße 17–19
14195 Berlin
Germany
…
Dated: 18 April 2023
[signed] [signed]
H.E. Ms Meredith Kirby H.E. Mr Carmelo Nvono-Ncá
For the Government of For the Government of the
the Republic of the Republic of Equatorial
Marshall Islands Guinea

M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (NO. 2) (ORDER 27 APRIL 2023) 216
3. Whereas the minutes of consultations agreed between the Marshall
Islands and Equatorial Guinea on 18 April 2023 and attached to the Special
Agreement read:
Consultations by the President of the Tribunal
with representatives of
the Republic of the Marshall Islands
and the Republic of Equatorial Guinea,
under article 3 of annex VII
to the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea
Minutes of consultations
1. At the invitation of the President of the International Tribunal for
the Law of the Sea, delegations from the Republic of the Marshall Islands
(hereinafter “the Marshall Islands”) and the Republic of Equatorial
Guinea (hereinafter “Equatorial Guinea”) participated in consultations
with the President under Annex VII to the Convention, held on 18 April
2023, at the premises of the Tribunal in Hamburg, Germany, on matters
relating to the arbitration proceedings instituted by the Marshall Islands
in the dispute between the Marshall Islands and Equatorial Guinea con-
cerning the M/T “Heroic Idun” and her crew, including the possible trans-
fer of the dispute to a special chamber of the Tribunal under article 15,
paragraph 2, of the Statute of the Tribunal.
2. The composition of the delegations was as follows:
For the Marshall Islands:
H.E. Ms Meredith Kirby, Deputy Commissioner of Maritime Affairs,
Agent;
Ms Joanne Moody, Solicitor, Tatham & Co;
Ms Penelope Nevill, Counsel, Twenty Essex Chambers;
Mr Paolo Busco, Counsel, Twenty Essex Chambers.
For Equatorial Guinea:
H.E. Mr Carmelo Nvono-Ncá, Ambassador, Agent;
H.E. Mr Juan Olo, Counselor;
Sir Michael Wood, Counsel, Twenty Essex Chambers;
Mr Jean-Charles Tchikaya, State Attorney;
Mr Adalberto Ncogo, State Attorney.

M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (NO. 2) (ORDER 27 APRIL 2023) 217
3. During the consultations, the parties agreed to transfer the arbitral
proceedings instituted by the Marshall Islands in the dispute between
the Marshall Islands and Equatorial Guinea concerning the M/T “Heroic
Idun” and her crew to a special chamber of the Tribunal to be formed
pursuant to article 15, paragraph 2, of the Statute.
4. The proceedings of the special chamber shall be governed by the
provisions contained in the Statute and the Rules of the Tribunal.
5. The parties agreed that the special chamber to be formed pursu-
ant to article 15, paragraph 2, of the Statute shall be composed of five
members, two of whom will be judges ad hoc chosen by the parties in
accordance with article 17 of the Statute of the Tribunal. The composi-
tion of the special chamber will be determined by the Tribunal with the
approval of the parties. In this respect, the parties have agreed on the
following names:
Judge Albert J. Hoffmann
Judge María Teresa Infante Caffi
Judge Kathy-Ann Brown
The judges ad hoc shall be Mr Gudmundur Eiriksson (nominated by the
Marshall Islands) and Mr Philippe Couvreur (nominated by Equatorial
Guinea).
…
4. Whereas the Special Agreement provides that delivery on 18 April 2023
of an original of the Special Agreement and Notification to the Registry of the
Tribunal shall constitute the notification contemplated in article 55 of the
Rules; and whereas an original copy of the Special Agreement was delivered to
Registrar of the Tribunal on that day;
5. Whereas the Special Agreement states that the Marshall Islands has
appointed H.E. Ms Meredith Kirby as Agent and Equatorial Guinea has
appointed H.E. Mr Carmelo Nvono-Ncá as Agent;
6. Whereas the Marshall Islands and Equatorial Guinea are States Parties to
the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea;

M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (NO. 2) (ORDER 27 APRIL 2023) 218
7. Whereas the Tribunal has before it a request by the two States Parties
that “the dispute concerning the M/T “Heroic Idun” and her crew” be heard and
determined by a special chamber to be formed under article 15, paragraph 2, of
the Statute;
8. Whereas, in the Special Agreement, the Parties conveyed their views
regarding the composition of the special chamber of the Tribunal;
9. Whereas, in the Special Agreement, the Marshall Islands notified the
Tribunal of its choice of Mr Gudmundur Eiriksson to sit as judge ad hoc in
the special chamber, and Equatorial Guinea notified the Tribunal of its choice
of Mr Philippe Couvreur to sit as judge ad hoc in the special chamber; and
whereas no objection to these choices appears to the Tribunal;
THE TRIBUNAL,
Unanimously,
1. Decides to accede to the request of the Marshall Islands and Equatorial
Guinea to form a special chamber of five judges to deal with “the dispute
concerning the M/T “Heroic Idun” and her crew”;
2. Determines, with the approval of the Parties, the composition of the
Special Chamber formed to deal with this case as follows:
President Hoffmann
Judges Infante Caffi
Brown
Judges ad hoc Eiriksson
Couvreur
3. Declares that the Special Chamber as composed above is duly constituted;
4. Decides that the proceedings before the Special Chamber shall be gov-
erned by the provisions contained in the Statute and the Rules;
5. Decides that the quorum required for meetings of the Special Chamber is
three members of the Special Chamber;

M/T “HEROIC IDUN” (NO. 2) (ORDER 27 APRIL 2023) 219
6. Reserves the subsequent procedure for further decision by the Special
Chamber.
Done in English and in French, both texts being equally authoritative, in
the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg, this twenty-seventh day of April, two
thousand and twenty-three, in three copies, one of which will be placed in the
archives of the Tribunal and the others transmitted to the Government of the
Marshall Islands and to the Government of Equatorial Guinea, respectively.
(signed)
Albert Hoffmann,
President
(signed)
Ximena Hinrichs Oyarce,
Registrar
court: International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)
